name: De Winter T-waves
identifyBy: Upsloping ST depression ≥1mm at J-point in V1–V6 (not classic ST elevation)
Followed immediately by tall, symmetric, hyperacute T waves (positive)
Absent or minimal ST elevation in V1–V6
Pattern is static - does not evolve like classic STEMI (does not develop Q waves acutely)

measurements.stSegment: Upsloping depression 1–3mm V1–V6 + hyperacute T waves

management: Treat as STEMI equivalent - activate cath lab immediately
Urgent evacuation - door-to-balloon target same as STEMI
This pattern indicates proximal LAD occlusion equivalent to anterior STEMI
pearls: De Winter T-waves are present in ~2% of acute LAD occlusions - easily missed because there is no classic ST elevation
The upsloping ST depression IS the key - it goes down at the J-point then T wave shoots up
Pattern may be present on the first ECG and does not evolve - serial ECGs may not show progression
Reciprocal ST elevation in aVR is a supporting clue
Reperfusion may convert De Winter pattern to typical anterior STEMI pattern
differentials: Early repolarisation (no ST depression, different morphology)
Hyperkalaemia (peaked T waves but different distribution)
